# caveui

> caveui is a community-driven, open-source catalog of beautiful, copy-friendly Jetpack Compose components for Android, built on top of Material 3. Components are copy-paste (you own the source) — there is no caveui dependency to install. The docs site is a static Next.js app; every component has a live preview (in an Android device mockup), light/dark theming, and copy-paste Kotlin.

## About

- Built on **Material 3** — components are plain Jetpack Compose customized on top of M3 (e.g. `Button`, `OutlinedTextField`), never bespoke wrappers.
- **Copy-paste**: copy the Kotlin from any component page into your project; make sure Material 3 (via the Compose BOM) is on your classpath.
- **MIT licensed**, no account, no backend.
- Repository: https://github.com/Jumnert/CaveUI
- Site: https://ui.jumnert.dev

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/introduction/): What caveui is and its principles.
- [Installation](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/installation/): Add Material 3 and copy a component.
- [Theming](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/theming/): Color, typography, shape and spacing tokens.
- [CLI](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/cli/): Command-line usage.
- [RTL](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/rtl/): Right-to-left layout support.
- [Skills](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/skills/): Agent skills for scaffolding components.
- [MCP Server](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/mcp-server/): Expose the registry to AI tools over MCP.
- [Registry](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/registry/): How components are described and served.
- [Forms](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/forms/): Building Compose forms with caveui inputs.
- [Changelog](https://ui.jumnert.dev/docs/changelog/): Notable changes.

## Components

- [All components](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/): The full gallery.
- [Buttons](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/buttons/): Actions in every emphasis, state and animation.
- [Cards](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/cards/): Surfaces for grouping content.
- [Inputs](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/inputs/): Text fields, search, password and more.
- [Badges](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/badges/): Status, counts and labels.
- [Toggles](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/toggles/): Switches and segmented controls.
- [Loaders](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/loaders/): Spinners, bars, skeletons and dots.
- [Icons](https://ui.jumnert.dev/components/icons/): Animated icons ready for Compose.

## Blocks

- [Blocks](https://ui.jumnert.dev/blocks/): Full-page sections composed from caveui components.

## Notes for agents

- Each component detail page lives at `/components/<category>/<id>/` and shows: a preview, an Installation note (Material 3 prerequisite), and the Usage Kotlin snippet to copy.
- Use the "Copy Page" action on a component page to grab an LLM-friendly markdown summary of that component.
- Some pages describe planned interfaces (a `npx caveui add` CLI and an MCP server) — treat those as intended/forthcoming rather than shipped.
